# Server Integration Testing with Scripttest
#
# This script demonstrates how to use scripttest to verify that
# different MCP server implementations conform to the same protocol.

# Create a test directory
cd ${WORK}
mkdir -p mcp-server-tests

# Create a standardized test suite for filesystem servers
cat > mcp-server-tests/filesystem_conformance.txt << 'EOF'
# MCP Filesystem Server Conformance Test
#
# This script tests if a filesystem server implementation
# conforms to the expected protocol.

# First, verify the server is running by checking initialization
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05"}}
stdout '"result"'
stdout '"serverInfo"'
stdout '"protocolVersion"'

# Test basic file operations

# Create a test file
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"write","params":{"path":"conformance_test.txt","content":"Test content for conformance testing"}}
stdout '"status": *"success"'

# Verify we can read the file
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"read","params":{"path":"conformance_test.txt"}}
stdout '"content": *"Test content for conformance testing"'

# Append to the file
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":4,"method":"append","params":{"path":"conformance_test.txt","content":"\nAppended content"}}
stdout '"status": *"success"'

# Create a directory
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":5,"method":"mkdir","params":{"path":"test_dir"}}
stdout '"status": *"success"'

# Write a file in the directory
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":6,"method":"write","params":{"path":"test_dir/file.txt","content":"File in directory"}}
stdout '"status": *"success"'

# List directory contents
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":7,"method":"list","params":{"path":"."}}
stdout '"entries"'
stdout '"conformance_test.txt"'
stdout '"test_dir"'

# Find files
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":8,"method":"find","params":{"path":".","pattern":"*.txt","recursive":true}}
stdout '"matches"'
stdout '"conformance_test.txt"'
stdout '"test_dir/file.txt"'

# Check file stat
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":9,"method":"stat","params":{"path":"conformance_test.txt"}}
stdout '"is_dir": false'
stdout '"size"'

# Test error handling - missing file
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":10,"method":"read","params":{"path":"nonexistent.txt"}}
! stdout '"result"'
stdout '"error"'

# Test file deletion
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":11,"method":"delete","params":{"path":"test_dir/file.txt"}}
stdout '"status": *"success"'

# Test directory deletion
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":12,"method":"delete","params":{"path":"test_dir","recursive":true}}
stdout '"status": *"success"'

# Cleanup test files
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":13,"method":"delete","params":{"path":"conformance_test.txt"}}
stdout '"status": *"success"'
EOF

# Create a reference implementation server script
cat > mcp-server-tests/reference_server.txt << 'EOF'
# Reference implementation of filesystem server

# Initialize response
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize"}
send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","serverInfo":{"name":"reference-fs-server","version":"1.0.0"},"capabilities":{}}}

# Write file
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"write","params":{"path":"*","content":"*"}}
exec mkdir -p $(dirname ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path})
exec echo "${REQUEST.params.content}" > ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}
send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"status":"success","path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","size":${#REQUEST.params.content},"message":"File written successfully"}}

# Append to file
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"append","params":{"path":"*","content":"*"}}
exec echo -n "${REQUEST.params.content}" >> ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}
set-var FILE_SIZE = $(wc -c < ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path} | tr -d ' ')
send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"status":"success","path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","size":${FILE_SIZE},"appended":${#REQUEST.params.content},"message":"Content appended successfully"}}

# Read file
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"read","params":{"path":"*"}}
exec test -f ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}
if ERRORLEVEL != 0
  send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"error":{"code":-32500,"message":"File not found: ${REQUEST.params.path}"}}
else
  set-var FILE_CONTENT = $(cat ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path})
  set-var FILE_SIZE = $(wc -c < ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path} | tr -d ' ')
  send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","content":"${FILE_CONTENT}","size":${FILE_SIZE},"encoding":"utf-8"}}
endif

# Make directory
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"mkdir","params":{"path":"*"}}
exec mkdir -p ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}
send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"status":"success","path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","message":"Directory created successfully"}}

# List directory
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"list","params":{"path":"*"}}
set-var DIR_ENTRIES = $(ls -1 ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path} | jq -R -s 'split("\n")[:-1] | map({"name": ., "is_dir": (if . == "" then false else (if (. | endswith("/")) then true else false end) end)})' 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")
set-var ENTRY_COUNT = $(echo ${DIR_ENTRIES} | jq 'length')
send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","entries":${DIR_ENTRIES},"count":${ENTRY_COUNT}}}

# Find files
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"find","params":{"path":"*","pattern":"*"}}
set-var RECURSIVE = ${REQUEST.params.recursive}
if RECURSIVE == true
  set-var FIND_RESULTS = $(find ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path} -name "${REQUEST.params.pattern}" | sed "s|${WORKDIR}/||" | jq -R -s 'split("\n")[:-1] | map({"path": ., "name": (. | split("/") | last), "is_dir": false})' 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")
else
  set-var FIND_RESULTS = $(find ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path} -maxdepth 1 -name "${REQUEST.params.pattern}" | sed "s|${WORKDIR}/||" | jq -R -s 'split("\n")[:-1] | map({"path": ., "name": (. | split("/") | last), "is_dir": false})' 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")
endif
set-var MATCH_COUNT = $(echo ${FIND_RESULTS} | jq 'length')
send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","pattern":"${REQUEST.params.pattern}","matches":${FIND_RESULTS},"count":${MATCH_COUNT}}}

# Stat file
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"stat","params":{"path":"*"}}
exec test -e ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}
if ERRORLEVEL != 0
  send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"error":{"code":-32500,"message":"Path not found: ${REQUEST.params.path}"}}
else
  set-var IS_DIR = $(if [ -d "${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}" ]; then echo "true"; else echo "false"; fi)
  set-var FILE_SIZE = $(if [ -f "${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}" ]; then wc -c < "${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}" | tr -d ' '; else echo "0"; fi)
  set-var FILE_SIZE_HUMAN = $(if [ -f "${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}" ]; then if [ ${FILE_SIZE} -gt 1024 ]; then echo $((${FILE_SIZE} / 1024))" KB"; else echo ${FILE_SIZE}" B"; fi; else echo "<DIR>"; fi)
  send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","name":"$(basename ${REQUEST.params.path})","is_dir":${IS_DIR},"size":"${FILE_SIZE_HUMAN}","size_bytes":${FILE_SIZE}}}
endif

# Delete file or directory
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"delete","params":{"path":"*"}}
set-var PATH_EXISTS = $(if [ -e "${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}" ]; then echo "true"; else echo "false"; fi)
if PATH_EXISTS == false
  send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"error":{"code":-32500,"message":"Path not found: ${REQUEST.params.path}"}}
else
  set-var IS_DIR = $(if [ -d "${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}" ]; then echo "true"; else echo "false"; fi)
  set-var RECURSIVE = ${REQUEST.params.recursive}
  if IS_DIR == true
    if RECURSIVE == true
      exec rm -rf ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}
      send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"status":"success","path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","type":"directory","message":"Successfully deleted directory: ${REQUEST.params.path}"}}
    else
      exec rmdir ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path} 2>/dev/null
      if ERRORLEVEL != 0
        send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"error":{"code":-32500,"message":"Directory not empty: ${REQUEST.params.path}"}}
      else
        send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"status":"success","path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","type":"directory","message":"Successfully deleted directory: ${REQUEST.params.path}"}}
      endif
    endif
  else
    exec rm ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}
    send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"status":"success","path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","type":"file","message":"Successfully deleted file: ${REQUEST.params.path}"}}
  endif
endif
EOF

# Create a test script that tests both servers
cat > mcp-server-tests/test_conformance.txt << 'EOF'
# Test both reference and alternative server against conformance tests

# 1. First, test the reference server
echo "===== Testing Reference Implementation ====="
mkdir -p reference_workdir
mcp-scripttest-server -script reference_server.txt -port 8086 -workdir reference_workdir &
sleep 1
cd ${WORK}

! stdout "FAIL"
# Run the conformance test against the reference server
MCP_SERVER=localhost:8086 go run ${WORK}/run_conformance_test.go
stdout "All tests passed"

# Clean up reference server
killall mcp-scripttest-server

# 2. Now test the implementation-under-test (using mcp-filesystem-server as an example)
echo "===== Testing Implementation Under Test ====="

# Start the real server
if [ -f "${WORK}/mcp-filesystem-server" ]; then
  ${WORK}/mcp-filesystem-server -port 8087 -dir ${WORK}/test_workdir &
else
  # If not available, use the mock implementation instead
  mkdir -p alt_workdir
  mcp-scripttest-server -script reference_server.txt -port 8087 -workdir alt_workdir &
fi
sleep 1

! stdout "FAIL"
# Run the conformance test against the implementation
MCP_SERVER=localhost:8087 go run ${WORK}/run_conformance_test.go
stdout "All tests passed"

# Clean up servers
killall mcp-filesystem-server 2>/dev/null
killall mcp-scripttest-server 2>/dev/null
EOF

# Create a Go program to run the conformance test
cat > run_conformance_test.go << 'EOF'
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"strings"
)

func main() {
	server := os.Getenv("MCP_SERVER")
	if server == "" {
		server = "localhost:8080"
	}
	
	// Run the conformance test
	cmd := exec.Command("mcp-test", "-server", server, "mcp-server-tests/filesystem_conformance.txt")
	cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
	cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
	
	err := cmd.Run()
	if err != nil {
		fmt.Printf("Conformance test failed: %v\n", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}
	
	fmt.Println("All tests passed")
}
EOF

# Create a simplified version of mcp-test for running the tests
cat > mcp-test << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Simple version of mcp-test for running conformance tests

SERVER="localhost:8080"
TEST_FILE=""

# Parse arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case $1 in
    -server)
      SERVER="$2"
      shift 2
      ;;
    *)
      TEST_FILE="$1"
      shift
      ;;
  esac
done

if [ -z "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
  echo "No test file specified"
  exit 1
fi

# Read the test file line by line
while IFS= read -r line; do
  # Skip comments and empty lines
  if [[ "$line" =~ ^# ]] || [[ -z "$line" ]]; then
    continue
  fi
  
  # Handle mcp-send commands
  if [[ "$line" =~ ^mcp-send ]]; then
    json=$(echo "$line" | sed 's/^mcp-send //')
    echo "Sending: $json"
    curl -s -X POST "http://$SERVER/mcp" \
         -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
         -d "$json" > response.json
    
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
      echo "Error sending request"
      exit 1
    fi
    
    cat response.json
    echo
  fi
  
  # Handle stdout checks
  if [[ "$line" =~ ^stdout ]]; then
    pattern=$(echo "$line" | sed 's/^stdout //' | sed 's/"//g')
    
    # Handle negated patterns
    if [[ "$pattern" =~ ^\! ]]; then
      actual_pattern=$(echo "$pattern" | sed 's/^\!//')
      if grep -q "$actual_pattern" response.json; then
        echo "ERROR: Found unexpected pattern: $actual_pattern"
        exit 1
      fi
    else
      if ! grep -q "$pattern" response.json; then
        echo "ERROR: Pattern not found: $pattern"
        exit 1
      fi
    fi
  fi
done < "$TEST_FILE"

# If we get here, all tests passed
echo "All tests passed"
exit 0
EOF

chmod +x mcp-test

# Run the test script
mcp-test mcp-server-tests/test_conformance.txt
stdout "===== Testing Reference Implementation ====="
stdout "All tests passed"
stdout "===== Testing Implementation Under Test ====="
stdout "All tests passed"
